content.problemStatement:
There are NNN cities connected by roads such that they form a tree-like structure.

Your task is to build exactly AiA_iAi​ towers in ithi^{th}ith city. One or multiple towers can be built in a city only if there is a brick factory present in that city.

Currently, only city 111 has a brick factory. To complete the project, you can do the following two types of operations:


Type 111: Construct a tower in a city that already has a brick factory.
Type 222: Construct a brick factory in a city such that any of its neighbouring cities already have a brick factory.

Note that two cities are considered as neighbouring cities if there is a road connecting them.
Find the number of **different ways** to complete the project such that the total number of operations performed is **minimum**. Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note: Two ways are considered different if the order and type of operations is not exactly the same for both ways.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains single integer NNN — the number of cities.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the number of towers to be built in ithi^{th}ith city.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines describe the roads. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N - 1N−1 lines contain two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting a road between city uiu_iui​ and city viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of **different ways** to complete the project in **minimum cost** modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.


Two ways are considered different if the order and type of operations is not exactly the same for both ways.
constraints: The sum of AiA_iAi​ over any test case is at least 111 and won't exceed 10610^6106.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
BiB_iBi​ represents construction of a brick factory in the city iii.
TiT_iTi​ represents construction of a tower in the city iii.

Test case 1: The only possible way to complete this project in minimum number of operations is B2B3T3B_2 B_3 T_3B2​B3​T3​.
Test case 2: There are 333 possible ways to complete this project in minimum number of operations: B2T2T1B_2 T_2 T_1B2​T2​T1​, B2T1T2B_2 T_1 T_2B2​T1​T2​ and T1B2T2 T_1 B_2 T_2T1​B2​T2​.
Test case 3: There are 333 possible ways to complete this project in minimum number of operations: B3T3T1B_3 T_3 T_1B3​T3​T1​, B3T1T3B_3 T_1 T_3B3​T1​T3​ and T1B3T3 T_1 B_3 T_3T1​B3​T3​.
